title: HTTP/2 Frames, Streams, HPACK, Flow Control, and Go Boundaries · Concurrent Code Workshop
summary: A protocol pairing room predicts byte, parser, state, ownership, and cleanup transitions before assembling the first HTTP/2 connection, stream, frame, and compression model for http/2 frames, streams, hpack, flow control, and go boundaries. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of HTTP/2 connection, stream, frame, and compression model while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evi…
objectives: Trace connection preface, SETTINGS exchange and acknowledgement, frame header, payload length, type, flags, stream identifier, and connection errors.
Apply idle, open, half-closed, closed, reserved, reset, and connection shutdown transitions independently per stream.
Map pseudo-fields and regular fields, enforce ordering and connection-field rules, bound HPACK tables and header lists, and avoid indexing secrets.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Commit to the mission model · Workshop H2 Preface Settings Frames
steps.instruction:
workshop case — A workshop protocol team handles case 4701 in a canary edge service advertising HTTP/3 with bounded fallback. Build deterministic Go evidence to combine HTTP/2 frame, stream, flow-control, and header-list bounds; preserve this operating constraint: one connection has exactly one read owner and one terminal close owner; then split every structural delimiter across a different read and retain the same result. Browser code models bounded byte and state decisions only. Sockets, listeners, live TLS, proxies, packet capture, fuzz campaigns, races, load, HTTP/2, HTTP/3, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: Commit to the mission model · Workshop H2 Preface Settings Frames.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for trace connection preface, settings exchange and acknowledgement, frame header, payload length, type, flags, stream identifier, and connection errors. For Commit to the mission model · Workshop H2 Preface Settings Frames, keep H2 Preface Settings Frames connected to executable Go behavior evidence.

Browser code models bounded byte and state decisions only. Sockets, listeners, live TLS, proxies, packet capture, fuzz campaigns, races, load, HTTP/2, HTTP/3, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.
